flashcards.back: A linear data structure where elements are stored in nodes containing data and a pointer to the next node.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of insertion at the beginning of a linked list?
What does the 'next' pointer of the last node point to?
flashcards.back: NULL, indicating the end of the list
A structure that contains a pointer to another instance of the same structure type.

flashcards.front: What is the main disadvantage of linked lists compared to arrays?
flashcards.back: No random access - elements must be accessed sequentially (O(n) time)

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of searching in a linked list?
flashcards.back: O(n) - Linear time in worst case

flashcards.front: What memory allocation method do linked lists use?
What are the three main types of linked lists?
flashcards.back: Singly linked list, Doubly linked list, Circular linked list

flashcards.front: What operation is needed before deleting a node from a linked list?
flashcards.back: Finding the node and maintaining a pointer to the previous node

flashcards.front: Why is memory deallocation important in linked list operations?
flashcards.back: To prevent memory leaks when nodes are deleted
